methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Reflex Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ravi Gondhalekar and Ben Kaplan. The company has raised $7M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private robotics firms (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable methodology here. Using total capital raised ($7M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $18M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Robotics & Manufacturing sector; while 11 peers operate in this space, fewer than three meet narrow filtering criteria, making sector-wide median benchmarking unreliable.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $15M (low) to $20M (high), with a median of $18M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x stage multiplier for this specific market and company profile, the degree to which public and private robotics comparables—spanning different subsegments and maturity profiles—inform Seed-stage pricing, and the strength of Reflex's IP and competitive positioning relative to the identified peer set. A fuller valuation would benefit from revenue traction, customer pipeline data, or capital efficiency metrics.
